business.headline: Atanu Chakraborty resigns as HDFC Bank part-time chairman over ethics issuesHDFC Bank part-time chairman Atanu Chakraborty resigned citing differences over values and ethics, with the RBI approving Keki Mistry as interim part-time chairman for a three-month period.

US Fed holds rates, flags higher inflation risks amid US-Iran war tensionsFederal Reserve kept rates unchanged, signaled one possible cut in 2026, and projected higher inflation amid Middle East tensions, while markets reacted cautiously and unemployment outlook remained steady.

Reliance Jio hires banks for IPO, no new capital to be raised: ReportThe IPO is expected to be structured as an "offer for sale" (OFS). This means existing investors will sell a part of their stake to the public, rather than the company issuing new shares.

Hidden reasons why life insurance claims get delayed or rejectedFiling a life insurance claim should be a straightforward process, especially during difficult times. Yet, many families face unexpected delays or rejections, often due to small details they overlooked. What really goes wrong?

Russian oil tanker bound for China diverts to India's New Mangalore PortA Russian oil tanker, Aqua Titan, originally bound for China, has diverted course in the South China Sea and is now headed to India's New Mangalore port, set to arrive on March 21st.

7 China-bound Russian oil tankers make U-turn, head to IndiaThe rerouting of these vessels carrying Russian oil comes after India received a temporary waiver from the US to buy 'sanctioned' Russian oil currently stranded at sea.

Iran-US war impact may push up medicine prices in IndiaPharma industry bodies have already raised the issue with the government, sending an emergency report highlighting the risk to raw material supplies.

Why borrowing now feels safer than saving for many IndiansToday, a growing number of Indians—especially younger, urban consumers—are embracing a very different model: spend first, borrow easily, and worry about repayment later.
Sensex ends 633 points higher, Nifty above 23,700; Infosys up 3%The S&P BSE Sensex jumped 633.29 points to close at 76,704.13, while the NSE Nifty50 added 196.65 points to end at 23,777.80.

Fixed deposits are boring, but are they the smartest bet right now?Fixed deposits may have fallen out of favour in recent years, but they never really disappeared. Even as investors chased higher returns in equities and gold, a large share of Indian savings stayed parked in banks.

Delhi High Court dismisses Peru's plea for sole rights over 'PISCO' nameThe case goes back to 2005, when Peru applied for GI protection for 'PISCO' in India. Chile opposed the move, claiming it had long used the name for its own grape-based spirit.

Nithin Kamath criticises banking apps for seeking unnecessary permissionsKamath referred to the Principle of Least Privilege (PoLP), which means apps should only ask for the minimum access required to function.

Why gold isn't rising despite Iran-US war and what it means for your moneyWar and uncertainty typically push gold prices higher, but the current trend tells a different story. Despite rising geopolitical tensions, gold is moving in a narrow range; why isn't it surging this time?

Sensex jumps 800 points: Why is the stock market rising today?The Sensex was up 825.79 points, or 1.09%, at 76,896.63, while the Nifty 50 gained 239.10 points, or 1.01%, to trade at 23,820.25 as of 11:44 AM. It may be noted that the Sensex has gained over 2,000 points this week after a sharp losing spree.

Air India restores long-grounded Boeing 777, completes revival of 30 aircraftThe aircraft, a Boeing 777-300 ER registered as VT-ALL, returned to commercial operations in March 2026 after remaining out of service since February 2020.

IRDAI forms working groups to address gaps in health insurance ecosystemThe development comes amid ongoing concerns over claim delays, disputes between hospitals and insurers, and rising medical costs, all of which have affected policyholders' experience despite the growth of health insurance coverage.

Sensex up 2,000 points this week: Why is market up despite ongoing Iran war?The rebound on Dalal Street this week comes even as tensions in the Middle East continue, raising questions about what is driving the market higher despite global uncertainty.

Insurance regulator plans Public Insurance Registry. What it means for policyholdersThe registry is envisioned as a unified digital infrastructure covering the entire lifecycle of an insurance policy, from issuance and servicing to claims, grievance redressal and dispute resolution.
Urban Company share price jumps 15%. Why is it rising today?At around 10:06 am, the Urban Company was trading 13.25% higher at Rs 124.70. Earlier in the trading session, the stock was up around 15%.
Infosys, TCS, HCLTech: Why are IT stocks rising today?As of 9:49 am, major IT stocks such as Infosys, TCS and HCLTech were up around 1–3%, emerging among the key contributors to the Nifty's gains.

entertainment.headline: Nora Fatehi's KD song Sarse Ninna Seraga: Was Kannada version tamer than Hindi? The controversial song from the upcoming pan-Indian Kannada film KD - The Devil has drawn criticism for its reportedly vulgar lyrics. While the Hindi version drew maximum backlash, the debate over whether the original Kannada version was equally suggestive has been gaining momentum.

Kim Kardashian stumbles in towering 8-inch heels at Oscars afterparty. Watch Kim Kardashian's Vanity Fair Oscars Party 2026 arrival didn't go entirely as planned. A viral moment in towering heels has everyone talking. Here's what happened.

Divyanka Tripathi confirms pregnancy, says she hid it for six months Divyanka Tripathi confirmed her pregnancy and shared how she kept it a secret for six months. Here's what she shared about the due date, plans and more.

Doraemon director Shibayama Tsutomu dies at 84 of lung cancer Japanese animation figure Shibayama Tsutomu, known for his work on Doraemon, Lupin III, and other popular series, has died at the age of 84. His death was announced by Ajia-do Animation Works, a studio he co-founded, and a private funeral has already taken place.

Dhurandhar The Revenge review: Power, politics and Ranveer Singh who devours it allRelease Date:Wed Mar 18, 2026 Dhurandhar: The Revenge is loud, political, and deeply character-driven, refusing to play safe at any point. Ranveer Singh leads a film that knows exactly the impact it wants to leave behind.

Dhurandhar 2: Madras High Court issues order against illegal streaming of film A day before its scheduled release on March 19, Dhurandhar: The Revenge receives legal protection from the Madras High Court. The order aims to prevent illegal broadcast and streaming across the internet and cable platforms.

Love Insurance Kompany: Pradeep Ranganathan's romantic sci-fi set for April release Vignesh Shivan's Love Insurance Kompany, featuring Pradeep Ranganathan, locks in a new theatrical release date. After multiple delays, the romantic sci-fi drama will hit theatres in April 2026.

Aditya Dhar apologises as Dhurandhar 2 screenings falter in parts of India Aditya Dhar apologised for disruptions during Dhurandhar: The Revenge previews, as dubbed versions faced issues. Makers have offered refunds and assured fixes before the March 19 release.

Ram Charan hosts cricketer Tilak Varma on Peddi sets, actor gifts him signed bat T20 World Cup winner Tilak Varma recently visited the sets of Peddi, starring Ram Charan, bringing together cricket and cinema. He was warmly welcomed by the team, adding to the buzz ahead of the film's April 2026 release.

R Madhavan shares UPSC success story of labourer's son ahead of Dhurandhar 2 release R Madhavan shared a video of a Chennai labourer's son who cleared UPSC (Union Public Service Commission) exams at his first attempt. The actor will next be seen in Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar: The Revenge.

KD song Sarke Chunar: Corrected version of track out soon, singer Mangli is sorry Singer Mangli issued a public apology after the lyrics of her Sarke Chunar song in the film KD - The Devil drew heavy criticism. The singer confirmed that the song has been pulled off all platforms and a revised version will be released soon.
Naagin 7's viral patriotic scene sparks a wild meme fest. See here A scene from Naagin 7 featuring Priyanka Chahar Choudhary and Alice Kaushik has gone viral for its mix of patriotism and fantasy.

He is not running away: High Court on why Rajpal Yadav won't be sent back to custody The Delhi High Court on Wednesday declined to send Rajpal Yadav back to custody in the ongoing cheque bounce case, despite vacating an interim order that had earlier suspended his sentence.

Men block cab, threaten Nimisha Nair in Mumbai; actor shares video Actor Nimisha Nair shared a disturbing harassment incident in Mumbai after two men allegedly blocked her cab and tried to intimidate her. The actor said that she experienced a similar incident earlier this year.

ZEE5, JioHotstar, SonyLIV this week: Chiraiya, Kasargod Embassy and more This week's regional OTT lineup brings a mix of powerful narratives, from rural resistance and social questioning to crime and historical drama.

Boney Kapoor loses 26 kg, says Sridevi would have loved to see him now Producer Boney Kapoor opened up about losing 26 kg and shares an emotional note about Sridevi. His latest post offered a glimpse into a deeply personal journey.

Dhurandhar The Revenge: Six burning questions we'll finally get answers to From Hamza's past to the truth behind key characters, Dhurandhar: The Revenge looks set to finally answer the many questions left behind by the first film. Directed by Aditya Dhar, the film stars Ranveer Singh, Arjun Rampal, R Madhavan, Rakesh Bedi and Sara Arjun among others.

Spider-Man: Brand New Day trailer: Tom Holland faces mayhem in a darker new era The trailer for Tom Holland's Spider-Man: Brand New Day was released on March 18. The Phase 6 solo-hero-film from Marvel Studios depicts a darker new era.

KD - The Devil director defends Sarse Ninna song, urges to watch full video first The lyrical video of Sarse Ninna Seraga Sarse from KD: The Devil has sparked controversy online, with criticism centred on its lyrics, visuals and portrayal of women. Director Prem has responded, defending the song's use of metaphor and expressing confidence in the Censor Board.

general news.headline: Israel won't hit Iran gas field again: Trump warns Tehran against striking QatarIsrael will no more attack Iran's South Pars gas field, US knew nothing: Trump

Live: Iran hits Gulf energy sectors after Israel strikes on South Pars gas fieldUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The United States was informed in advance of Israel's strike on Iran's South Pars gas field but did not take part, as global oil prices surged on fears of retaliation. Hours later, a missile hit Qatar's key gas facility, causing significant damage and prompting Doha to expel some Iranian embassy officials. Israel also killed Iran's intelligence minister, further destabilising its leadership, as Tehran warned of retaliation. Meanwhile, an Iranian missile struck a building in the occupied West Bank, killing three people and injuring at least 13 — the first fatalities there since the Iran war began. Stay tuned to IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

Mamata vs Suvendu: Is Bhabanipur the litmus test of political supremacy in Bengal?As West Bengal gears up for the 2026 Assembly elections, the Bhabanipur contest transforms into a fierce test of political dominance, with Mamata Banerjee and Suvendu Adhikari battling for urban supremacy and voter trust.

Has Israel killed Larijani to keep the Iran war going?The US-Israel war was meant to decapitate Iran's hardline leadership, but the killing of the "pragmatic" Ali Larijani might have done the opposite. By eliminating consensus-builder Larijani, the Israelis might have removed any scope of talks and pushed Iran into deeper hardline control. This could be a ploy to keep the war going.

The million-year accident: Biography of Strait of Hormuz, chokepoint that rules worldBorn of colliding continents and vanished seas, the Strait of Hormuz is the million‑year accident that turned buried oceans into oil, and a thirty‑mile gap in the rocks into the world's most dangerous chokepoint.

Pakistan among biggest nuclear threats to US, says Intel chief Tulsi GabbardGabbard said that the U.S. intelligence community assesses a sharp rise in the number of missiles capable of striking the United States. According to estimates, the figure is expected to grow to over 16,000 by 2035, a significant increase from the current assessed number of around 3,000.

Tenant vs landlord: How India's new rental rules change the balanceRenting a home in India has long been a negotiation-heavy process, with unclear rules and uneven power between tenants and landlords. A new rental framework based on the Model Tenancy Act, 2021 aims to bring more structure, clarity, and balance to how homes are rented.

How a loyal Congress MP from Assam was forced to join the BJPPradyut Bordoloi was a dyed-in-the-wool Congressi who vowed to never leave the party. The physical attack on him in 2025, and the Congress embracing "communal elements" pushed the Lok Sabha MP into joining the BJP. Bordoloi's exit from the Congress after three decades is significant for the party and Assam's politics.

Garbage dump to green cover: 3-km canal in Tamil Nadu revived into mangrove forestAround 600 villagers removed 750 kg of waste, following which 3,000 mangrove saplings were planted under a Rs 69 lakh project. The restoration has improved water flow in the Pichavaram mangroves and is supporting the livelihoods of Irula fishing families.

Deve Gowda on Kharge's marriage with Modi quip: Had abusive relation with CongressCiting the 2018 Karnataka government formation and the 2019 coalition collapse, Gowda said the Congress "walked away" from the alliance, leaving him with no choice but to seek a more stable partnership.

sports.headline: Virat Kohli has a squad of fighters: AB de Villiers backs RCB to defend IPL crown IPL 2026: Former South African batter AB de Villiers backed RCB to win consecutive titles, praising their strong, balanced squad and saying the team is no longer solely reliant on Virat Kohli.

PCB source reveals Babar Azam and Fakhar Zaman may not have been fully fit when they were selected for Pakistan's T20 World Cup squad, with the PCB expected to investigate after concerns were raised by senior selector Aaqib Javed and the board's medical panel.

Spin-friendly pitch can be KKR's key to success in IPL: Faf du Plessis IPL: Kolkata Knight Riders' key to success in the Indian Premier League will depend on the kind of pitches that they make, believes Faf du Plessis. KKR endured a difficult season at home last time.

India drawn with China in Thomas and Uber Cups 2026 India have been handed tough draws in both the Thomas Cup and Uber Cup 2026 after being grouped with defending champions China. The BWF announced the draws for the competition, which is set to held  between April 24 and May 3.

FC Barcelona will be Hansi Flick's last coaching job Hansi Flick says Barcelona will be his last club as a coach, ahead of a crucial Champions League second leg against Newcastle at Camp Nou on Wednesday.

SRH name Ishan Kishan as stand-in captain in absence of Pat Cummins IPL 2026: Wicketkeeper batter Ishan Kishan has been named the captain of SunRisers Hyderabad in the absence of Pat Cummins, who is set to miss the first few IPL games for the franchise. Kishan's promotion comes in the backdrop of his sensational outing in the T20 World Cup 2026.

Salman Agha, Mehidy Hasan Miraz soar in ICC rankings after stellar ODI seriesSalman Agha and Mehidy Hasan Miraz headlined the latest ICC rankings update after Bangladesh's 2-1 ODI series win over Pakistan, with Salman rising to No.9 among batters and Mehidy climbing to No.7 among bowlers.

Is Bumrah set to lose Rs 2 crore despite T20 World Cup heroics?Fast bowler Jasprit Bumrah was one of the standout performers in India's T20 World Cup campaign. Despite his exceptional outing, sections of the Indian media have reported that he could face a pay cut.

The decision was made on Tuesday, March 17, to have the title handed over to Morocco after the federation deemed that Senegal forfeited the game.

Lalit Modi's message to SunRisers group: I know a thing or two about managing opticsLalit Modi has offered to help the SunRisers group as their franchise faces heat over signing Pakistan spinner Abrar Ahmed for The Hundred 2026.

World Cup teaches you how important mental strength is: Abhishek SharmaIndia opener Abhishek Sharma has opened up on his struggles during the T20 World Cup 2026. The batter has revealed how advice from Yuvraj Singh, Gautam Gambhir and Suryakumar Yadav helped him return to form.

DC captain Axar given warning by Pujara ahead of IPL 2026: Don't be extra casualFormer India cricketer Cheteshwar Pujara told Axar Patel not to get too casual when it comes to being the captain of Delhi Capitals in IPL 2026. Axar took over the reins last season for the Capitals.

Despite Ashes axe, Ollie Pope remains hopeful of England Test team returnOllie Pope remains hopeful of an England comeback after being dropped during the Ashes 2025-26, as he aims to regain form in county cricket. Pope admitted that him being dropped during the series against Australia was the right call in the end.

Can bat, bowl, field and now fly: Phillips shows pilot skills, video goes viralGlenn Phillips flaunted his skills as a pilot as he landed a small aircraft in Auckland. Phillips has been vocal about his ambition of being a pilot and considers it to be a career option after retiring from cricket.

Sports ministry to review boycott stanceBangladesh sports minister Anamul Haque orders probe into the country's T20 World Cup boycott and BCB elections, plans talks with ICC over board's future and governance issues. Vinicius Jr hits brace as Real Madrid dump 10-man City out of Champions LeagueA brace from Vinicius Jr. was enough for Real Madrid to dump 10-man Manchester City out of the Champions League and book their place in the quarterfinals. Senegal lose AFCON title in bizarre decision overturn, Morocco crowned winnersMorocco has been awarded the 2026 African Cup of Nations title after the Confederation of ‌African Football (CAF) overturned the final win of Senegal. The CAF ruled that Senegal had forfeited their game after they left the field during the final following a controversial penalty being awarded to Morocco.

Riyan Parag Interview: Rajasthan captain looks to Premier League for inspirationIPL 2026: Riyan Parag is set to begin his first season as the full-time Rajasthan Royals captain. Before the start of the season, young Parag travelled to London, from where he brought back some valuable leadership lessons.

Emma Raducanu pulls out of Miami Open, continues struggling in challenging seasonBritish tennis star and 2021 US Open champion Emma Raducanu has withdrawn from the 2026 Miami Open due to illness and lingering post-viral symptoms, missing a chance to defend her 215 ranking points.

Did Kamran Akmal call Mohsin Naqvi a trophy chor after Bangladesh series loss?Former Pakistan wicketkeeper Kamran Akmal slammed the team's poor performance in the series against Bangladesh, calling Pakistan cricket a comedy series. His comments also indirectly referenced PCB chairman Mohsin Naqvi, linked to the Asia Cup controversy.

Pratika Rawal recalls emotional ride in Women's World Cup final: Didn't see last ballPratika Rawal described the Women's World Cup final against South Africa as an emotional whirlwind, saying she didn't even watch the last ball. The right-handed batter missed the final due to an ankle and knee injury.

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
